message:
test(fj-conformance): add value_deserializer fuzz target with serde-roundtrip oracle

New cargo-fuzz target covering Value's serde JSON deserialization
path. Value is part of the public IR surface (callers pass Values
through serialized requests, the dispatcher round-trips them
across rch boundaries) so any panic or roundtrip-asymmetry latent
in the deserializer is a real regression risk.

Harness shape:
- Bound input length at 16 KB to avoid burning fuzz budget on
  pathologically long inputs that mostly stress the JSON parser
  rather than Value's structure.
- Try serde_json::from_slice::<Value>(data); failures are silently
  skipped (most fuzz inputs are not valid JSON, that's expected).
- For successful deserialization, run a serialization roundtrip:
  to_vec + from_slice + assert_eq! against the original. This is
  the strongest property — any field that's silently dropped on
  serialize, or any normalization that's not idempotent, surfaces
  as a fuzz finding.
- For each successfully-roundtripped Value, exercise every public
  scalar accessor (dtype, as_scalar_literal, as_f64_scalar,
  as_i64_scalar, as_bool_scalar, as_complex128_scalar) and the
  tensor accessor (as_tensor → len, is_empty). The result of each
  accessor call is dropped — we're fuzzing for panic/abort, not
  for return-value correctness — but exercising every accessor
  catches branch-arm panics in the variant dispatch that the
  surface-level roundtrip wouldn't.

Wired into crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml as the
"value_deserializer" bin alongside the existing fj-conformance
fuzz targets (test/doc/bench all false to match the project's
fuzz-target convention).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -37,6 +37,13 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "value_deserializer"
+path = "fuzz_targets/value_deserializer.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "shape_inference_engine"
 path = "fuzz_targets/shape_inference_engine.rs"
